channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Strategies to control the geometry and symmetry around lanthanide centres for tailored luminescence and magnetism
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 17 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69445-6

The luminescent and magnetic properties of trivalent lanthanides are indispensable for many emerging technologies, but exacting fine control over these properties requires an understanding of the coordination geometry and site symmetry of Ln3+ centres. Here, the authors leverage an analysis of 12,670 eight-coordinate Ln3+ centres in the Cambridge Structural Database to prescribe useful strategies for controlling coordination geometry.
item.dc:title: Strategies to control the geometry and symmetry around lanthanide centres for tailored luminescence and magnetism
item.dc:creator: Thomas E. Karpiuk
Daniel B. Leznoff

item.title: Author Correction: Collapse of density wave and emergence of superconductivity in pressurized-La4Ni3O10 evidenced by ultrafast spectroscopy
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69758-6

Author Correction: Collapse of density wave and emergence of superconductivity in pressurized-La4Ni3O10 evidenced by ultrafast spectroscopy
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Collapse of density wave and emergence of superconductivity in pressurized-La4Ni3O10 evidenced by ultrafast spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: Shuxiang Xu
Hao Wang
Mengwu Huo
Deyuan Hu
Qiong Wu
Li Yue
Dong Wu
Meng Wang
Tao Dong
Nanlin Wang

item.title: Photogated two conductive pathways of donor-acceptor Stenhouse adducts in single-molecule junctions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69459-0

Achieving simultaneous control of multiple transport pathways within single-molecule junctions remains challenging. Here, Sun et al. report light-controlled modulation of two distinct conductive pathways in single donor-acceptor Stenhouse adduct molecule with three thiomethyl anchoring sites.
item.dc:title: Photogated two conductive pathways of donor-acceptor Stenhouse adducts in single-molecule junctions
item.dc:creator: Fanxi Sun
Shengqing Jiang
Hanjun Zhang
Rui Wang
Yu Ji
Songjun Hou
Maolin Zhang
Gaolu Zhu
Tianfang Shi
Jiayu Li
Yuantao Zheng
Wenshu Liu
Yangyang Pan
Hao Luo
Xu Deng
Yonghao Zheng
Chen Wei
Dongsheng Wang
